# Also check if there was a red block in bottom panel initially
print("\nInitial bottom panel blocks:")
for n in initial_seg['nodes']:

# Check current bottom panel

</parameter>
</function>

sections.content:
Very interesting! Looking at the initial state:
- Right panel: R at [19, 42], b at [25, 42], N at [31, 42]
- Current right panel: R at [13, 42], b at [19, 42], N at [25, 42]
The blocks moved UP! Initially:
- Red was at row 19
- Blue was at row 25
- Green was at row 31
Now:
- Red is at row 13
- Blue is at row 19
- Green is at row 25
So the blocks moved up by 6 rows each! And the order changed from R, b, N to R, b, N (same order but shifted up).
Wait, but the bottom panel order is R, N, b (red, green, blue). The right panel order is R, b, N (red, blue, green). These don't match!
So the goal might be to rearrange the right panel blocks to match the bottom panel order: red, green, blue. Currently the right panel is red, blue, green. I need to swap blue and green.
But how? The blocks have moved up when I called ACTION7. Maybe ACTION7 moves the block up when the path is touching it? Let me check:
- Initially red was at row 19, now at row 13 (moved up 6)
- Initially blue was at row 25, now at row 19 (moved up 6)
- Initially green was at row 31, now at row 25 (moved up 6)
So all 3 blocks moved up by 6 rows. This happened because I called ACTION7 on each one. But the order is still wrong.
Wait, let me think about this differently. Maybe the blocks need to be in the same vertical positions as the bottom panel blocks? The bottom panel blocks are at row 57. That doesn't make sense.
Or maybe the goal is to have the blocks in the same order as the bottom panel. The bottom panel shows: red, green, blue (left to right). The right panel shows: red, blue, green (top to bottom). I need to swap blue and green on the right panel.
But how do I swap them? Maybe I need to move the blocks using the path? Let me think...
Actually, looking at the initial positions more carefully:
- Initial: R at row 19, b at row 25, N at row 31 (spacing of 6 rows)
- Current: R at row 13, b at row 19, N at row 25 (spacing of 6 rows)
The blocks moved up, maintaining the same order. Maybe ACTION7 moves the touched block up by one "slot" (6 rows)?
If that's the case, maybe I need to move the blocks to specific positions. Let me check if there's a target position indicated somewhere.
Actually, wait. Let me look at the initial state again. The right panel blocks were at rows 19, 25, 31. Now they're at rows 13, 19, 25. The top block (red) moved from 19 to 13, which is 6 rows up. But there's no block above row 13. Maybe the blocks can only move up to certain positions?
Let me think about the bottom panel order: red, green, blue. The right panel should match this order. Currently right panel is red, blue, green. To get red, green, blue, I need to move green above blue.
Maybe I need to move green up past blue? If ACTION7 moves the touched block up, and I touch green while blue is above it, maybe green will move up and swap with blue?
Let me try: move DOWN to green, extend RIGHT, ACTION7. This should move green up.
